steps.title: Set up the tangent inequality
steps.content:
We know that for a right triangle, $\tan\theta = \dfrac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} = \dfrac{22.7}{x}$.
The law requires $\tan\theta \le \dfrac{1}{12}$, so $$\dfrac{22.7}{x} \le \dfrac{1}{12}.$$ Since $x>0$, multiply both sides by $12x$: $$22.7 \cdot 12 \le x.$$ That simplifies to $x \ge 272.4$.

steps.title: Compute the least possible x
steps.content:
From $x \ge 12 \times 22.7$, compute $12 \times 22.7 = 272.4$.
Therefore the least possible value of $x$ is \$272.4$ inches.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
